title: Podman 4.0.3 creates machine with Podman 4.0.2
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Podman 4.0.3 creates machine with Podman 4.0.2

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `brew install podman`

2. `podman machine init && podman machine start`

3. `podman version`

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.3
API Version:  4.0.3
Go Version:   go1.18
Built:        Fri Apr  1 11:28:59 2022
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.2
API Version:  4.0.2
Go Version:   go1.16.14
Built:        Thu Mar  3 09:58:50 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Expected Server Podman v4.0.3

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I'm not sure if it's a Podman or a Homebrew issue, to be honest.

Also tried to build from source with `brew install --build-from-source podman` to rule out a Homebrew bottling issue.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.3
API Version:  4.0.3
Go Version:   go1.18
Built:        Fri Apr  1 11:28:59 2022
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.2
API Version:  4.0.2
Go Version:   go1.16.14
Built:        Thu Mar  3 09:58:50 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

M1 MacBook Pro, macOS 12.3

comments.body:
@baude Did you update your coreos images with 4.0.3?

@shanesmith Generally speaking the version on the host and in the VM are not in sync. The version inside is managed by the underlying os inside the VM.
I see. Where can I track the version that's in coreos?

podman version 

Should show you the versions of the client and server.

Sorry, I meant on the web. 😅

Is there a site where I can see what's currently in coreos and where I could possibly help bump it along? =)

The issue right now is we are not using the upstream coreos until Fedora 36 releases.   I am not sure if this is easily checked on the fedora coreos release.  But I know it is difficult with our lookaside cache machine.  I will ping the maintainer to see what the state of the update is.
I'm working on new releases. I have the intel one but I am having trouble producing the aarch64 ones due to trouble finding open aarch64 hardware.  Stay tuned.
